21.correct: The term of the Attorney General of India is NOT fixed by the Constitution - the AG holds office during the pleasure of the President.

wrong.A: It is not a fixed 3-year term.
wrong.B: It is not a fixed 5-year term.
wrong.C: There is no fixed retirement age of 65 specified for the AG.

21.fact: The Attorney General is the highest law officer of India (Article 76).

22.correct: During Rongali Bihu, 'Kutum Bihu' is the day when people traditionally visit relatives and exchange gifts.

wrong.A: Goru Bihu is the day for honouring cattle.
wrong.B: Manuh Bihu is the day for personal cleansing and respect to elders.
wrong.D: 'Bohagi Bihu' is not the specific gift-exchange day.

22.fact: Rongali (Bohag) Bihu marks the Assamese New Year and the spring season.

23.correct: Rajasthan is known for the folk music and dance forms Ghoomar and Kalbeliya.

wrong.C: Himachal Pradesh is known for Nati.
wrong.D: Sikkim has its own distinct folk traditions.

23.fact: Kalbeliya is on UNESCO's list of Intangible Cultural Heritage.

24.correct: 'The Alchemist' was written by the Brazilian author Paulo Coelho.

wrong.B: Dan Brown wrote 'The Da Vinci Code'.
wrong.C: Gabriel Garcia Marquez wrote 'One Hundred Years of Solitude'.

24.fact: 'The Alchemist' (1988) is one of the best-selling books of all time.

25.correct: The small intestine receives digestive secretions from the liver (bile) and the pancreas (pancreatic juice).

wrong.A: The kidney is an excretory organ, not a digestive-secretion source.
wrong.C: The spleen is not a digestive gland.
wrong.D: The gall bladder stores bile, and the lungs are respiratory organs.

25.fact: Bile emulsifies fats, while pancreatic juice contains enzymes for all major nutrients.

26.correct: Morarji Desai is the only person to have received both India's Bharat Ratna and Pakistan's highest honour, the Nishan-e-Pakistan.

wrong.A: Atal Bihari Vajpayee received the Bharat Ratna but not the Nishan-e-Pakistan.
wrong.C: P.V. Narasimha Rao did not receive both.
wrong.D: Lal Bahadur Shastri did not receive the Nishan-e-Pakistan.

26.fact: Morarji Desai was India's Prime Minister from 1977 to 1979.

27.correct: The Tehri Dam is built on the Bhagirathi river (a headstream of the Ganga) in Uttarakhand.

wrong.B: It is not on the Yamuna.
wrong.C: It is on the Bhagirathi, a tributary that forms the Ganga, not the main Ganga directly.
wrong.D: It is not on the Godavari.

27.fact: The Tehri Dam is one of the tallest dams in India.

28.correct: Mahesh Bhupathi became the first Indian to win a Grand Slam title in 1997 (mixed doubles at the French Open).

wrong.C: 1998 is after his first Grand Slam.

28.fact: Mahesh Bhupathi won the 1997 French Open mixed doubles with Rika Hiraki.

29.correct: The companion cell is part of the phloem, NOT the xylem; xylem consists of tracheids, vessels, xylem parenchyma and xylem fibres.

wrong.C: Xylem parenchyma is a xylem component.

29.fact: Companion cells assist the sieve-tube elements of the phloem in transporting food.

30.correct: The decision to establish a permanent BIMSTEC Secretariat was taken at the Third BIMSTEC Summit.

wrong.A: It was not the first summit.
wrong.B: It was not the second summit.
wrong.D: It was not the fourth summit.

30.fact: The BIMSTEC Secretariat is located in Dhaka, Bangladesh.

31.correct: India's women's kabaddi team won its first Asian Games gold medal in 2010 (Guangzhou), when women's kabaddi debuted at the Games.

wrong.B: 2006 did not feature women's kabaddi at the Asian Games.
wrong.C: 2014 - India also won, but not the first.

31.fact: India dominated kabaddi at the Asian Games for many years.

32.correct: The octet rule (achieving eight electrons in the outer shell) applies well up to elements like calcium (the lighter main-group elements).

wrong.B: Potassium is also light, but the octet rule's clean applicability is usually cited up to calcium.
wrong.C: Thorium is a heavy element where the octet rule does not apply simply.
wrong.D: Hydrogen follows a duplet (2-electron) rule, not the octet.

32.fact: The octet rule has many exceptions, especially for heavier and transition elements.

33.correct: M.S. Swaminathan is known as the architect of India's Green Revolution and was awarded the Bharat Ratna in 2024.

wrong.A: Verghese Kurien led the White (milk) Revolution.
wrong.C: Norman Borlaug led the global Green Revolution but is not Indian.
wrong.D: C. Subramaniam was the agriculture minister who supported it, but the 'architect' title is Swaminathan's.

33.fact: M.S. Swaminathan was posthumously conferred the Bharat Ratna in 2024.

34.correct: The primary objective of the Subhadra Yojana (Odisha) is to provide financial assistance to economically weaker women.

wrong.A: It is not a pension scheme for senior citizens.
wrong.C: It is not a renewable-energy scheme.
wrong.D: It is not a youth-employment scheme.

34.fact: Subhadra Yojana provides Rs 50,000 over five years to eligible women in Odisha.

35.correct: Under the Delhi Sultanate, introducing new crops like sugarcane and better irrigation led to diversification of farming and greater agricultural surplus.

wrong.A: It did not cause a decline in subsistence farming.
wrong.C: It did not increase dependence on imports.
wrong.D: It did not lead to abandoning traditional crops.

35.fact: Sultanate rulers like Firoz Shah Tughlaq built canals to expand irrigation.

36.correct: When a wire is stretched to double its length (volume staying constant), its resistance becomes 4 times the original, since R is proportional to the square of the length.

wrong.A: 4 ohm would be the original, not the new value.
wrong.B: 8 ohm corresponds to a 2x increase, not 4x.

36.fact: Stretching doubles the length and halves the area, so R increases by a factor of 2 x 2 = 4.

37.correct: The Mohorovicic discontinuity (the 'Moho') separates the Earth's crust from the mantle.

wrong.B: The Repetti discontinuity lies within the mantle.
wrong.C: The Conrad discontinuity is within the crust.
wrong.D: The Gutenberg discontinuity separates the mantle from the core.

37.fact: The Moho marks a sharp change in seismic-wave velocity between crust and mantle.

38.correct: Khan Abdul Ghaffar Khan was also known as 'Badshah Khan' (and 'Frontier Gandhi').

wrong.A: 'Baba-e-Qaum' is a title for Jinnah in Pakistan.

38.fact: Ghaffar Khan founded the non-violent Khudai Khidmatgar movement.

39.correct: Water protects crops from both frost and hot winds (e.g. by light irrigation, which moderates temperature near the plants).

wrong.B: Manure improves soil fertility but does not protect from frost/heat directly.
wrong.C: Starch is not used for crop protection.
wrong.D: Fertilizer adds nutrients but does not protect from frost and hot winds.

39.fact: Irrigating fields before a frosty night can protect crops, as water releases heat as it cools.

40.correct: According to the global remittance-inflow data, India received about 14.3% of global remittances in 2024 - the largest share of any country.
40.fact: India has been the world's top recipient of remittances for many years.
